name: Shvetashvatara Upanishad
summary: The Shvetashvatara Upanishad is unique in its theistic flavor, identifying Brahman with Rudra-Shiva. It teaches that the individual soul, the world, and God are distinct yet united, and that liberation comes through devotion and knowledge of the one God.

verses.sanskrit:
एको देवः सर्वभूतेषु गूढः सर्वव्यापी सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा |
कर्माध्यक्षः सर्वभूताधिवासः साक्षी चेता केवलो निर्गुणश्च ||६.११||
verses.transliteration:
eko devaḥ sarvabhūteṣu gūḍhaḥ sarvavyāpī sarvabhūtāntarātmā |
karmādhyakṣaḥ sarvabhūtādhivāsaḥ sākṣī cetā kevalo nirguṇaś ca ||6.11||
verses.english_translation: The one God, hidden in all beings, all-pervading, the inner Self of all beings, the overseer of all actions, dwelling in all beings, the witness, the pure consciousness, the one, without qualities.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एकः देवः — अद्वितीयः परमेश्वरः — सर्वभूतेषु गूढः — सर्वेषु प्राणिषु अन्तर्हितः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The one God — without a second — is hidden in all beings as their inner Self. He is the witness of all actions, pure consciousness, and beyond all qualities. Knowing this one God, one is freed from all bonds.

verses.sanskrit:
द्वा सुपर्णा सयुजा सखाया समानं वृक्षं परिषस्वजाते |
तयोरन्यः पिप्पलं स्वाद्वत्त्यनश्नन्नन्यो अभिचाकशीति ||४.६||
verses.transliteration:
dvā suparṇā sayujā sakhāyā samānaṃ vṛkṣaṃ pariṣasvajāte |
tayor anyaḥ pippalaṃ svādv atty anaśnann anyo abhicākaśīti ||4.6||
verses.english_translation: Two birds, companions always united, cling to the same tree. One eats the sweet fruit; the other, not eating, simply watches.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: द्वौ सुपर्णौ — जीवेश्वरौ। जीवः कर्मफलानि भुङ्क्ते। ईश्वरः साक्षी।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The two birds are the individual soul (jiva) and the Lord (Ishvara). The jiva eats the fruits of karma; the Lord is the witness. When the jiva recognizes its identity with the witnessing Lord, it is liberated.

verses.sanskrit:
भयादस्याग्निस्तपति भयात्तपति सूर्यः |
भयादिन्द्रश्च वायुश्च मृत्युर्धावति पञ्चमः ||६.३||
verses.transliteration:
bhayād asyāgnis tapati bhayāt tapati sūryaḥ |
bhayād indraś ca vāyuś ca mṛtyur dhāvati pañcamaḥ ||6.3||
verses.english_translation: Out of fear of Him, fire burns; out of fear of Him, the sun shines; out of fear of Him, Indra and Vayu, and death as the fifth, run.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अस्य — परमेश्वरस्य — भयात् — अनुशासनात् — अग्निः तपति। सर्वे देवाः तस्य आज्ञां पालयन्ति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Out of reverence and fear of the supreme Lord, fire burns, the sun shines, and all the cosmic forces perform their functions. The entire universe operates under His governance.

verses.sanskrit:
एको देवः सर्वभूतेषु गूढः सर्वव्यापी सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा |
कर्माध्यक्षः सर्वभूताधिवासः साक्षी चेता केवलो निर्गुणश्च ||६.११||
verses.transliteration:
eko devaḥ sarvabhūteṣu gūḍhaḥ sarvavyāpī sarvabhūtāntarātmā |
karmādhyakṣaḥ sarvabhūtādhivāsaḥ sākṣī cetā kevalo nirguṇaś ca ||6.11||
verses.english_translation: The one God, hidden in all beings, all-pervading, the inner Self of all beings, the overseer of all actions, dwelling in all beings, the witness, the pure consciousness, the one, without qualities.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एकः देवः — अद्वितीयः परमेश्वरः — सर्वभूतेषु गूढः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The one God is hidden in all beings as their inner Self. He is the witness of all actions, pure consciousness, and beyond all qualities. Knowing this one God, one is freed from all bonds.

verses.sanskrit:
एको रुद्रो न द्वितीयाय तस्थुर्य इमाँल्लोकानीशत ईशनीभिः |
प्रत्यङ्जनांस्तिष्ठति सञ्चुकोचान्तकाले संसृज्य विश्वा भुवनानि गोपाः ||३.२||
verses.transliteration:
eko rudro na dvitīyāya tasthur ya imāṃl lokān īśata īśanībhiḥ |
pratyañjanāṃs tiṣṭhati sañcukocāntakāle saṃsṛjya viśvā bhuvanāni gopāḥ ||3.2||
verses.english_translation: One Rudra — no second — rules these worlds with his powers. He stands within all people. Having created all worlds, he withdraws them at the end of time. He is the protector.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एकः रुद्रः — अद्वितीयः। न द्वितीयाय — द्वितीयः नास्ति। इमान् लोकान् ईशते — सर्वान् लोकान् नियच्छति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: There is only one Rudra — no second. He rules all worlds, stands within all beings, creates and dissolves all worlds. This is the theistic teaching of the Shvetashvatara — Brahman as the personal God Rudra-Shiva.

verses.sanskrit: युञ्जानः प्रथमं मनस्तत्त्वाय सविता धियः | अग्नेर्ज्योतिर्निचाय्य पृथिव्या अध्याभरत् ||२.१||
verses.transliteration: yuñjānaḥ prathamaṃ manas tattvāya savitā dhiyaḥ | agner jyotir nicāyya pṛthivyā adhy ābharat ||2.1||
verses.english_translation: First yoking the mind to the truth, Savita (the Sun) of the intellects, having perceived the light of fire, brought it from the earth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: युञ्जानः — योगम् अभ्यसन् — प्रथमम् मनः — तत्त्वाय — ब्रह्मणि — युञ्जीत।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The yoga practice begins with yoking the mind to the truth (Brahman). The Sun (Savita) represents the illuminating power of consciousness. The yogi first controls the mind, then perceives the inner light of the Self.

verses.sanskrit: यस्य देवे परा भक्तिर्यथा देवे तथा गुरौ | तस्यैते कथिता ह्यर्थाः प्रकाशन्ते महात्मनः ||६.२३||
verses.transliteration: yasya deve parā bhaktir yathā deve tathā gurau | tasyaite kathitā hy arthāḥ prakāśante mahātmanaḥ ||6.23||

word_meanings.meaning: as to God, so to the teacher
to that one these meanings shine forth

verses.english_translation: To one who has supreme devotion to God, and to the teacher as to God — to that great soul, these meanings shine forth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: देवे परा भक्तिः — ईश्वरे उत्कृष्टा भक्तिः। गुरौ अपि तथैव। तस्य एते अर्थाः — उपनिषदर्थाः — प्रकाशन्ते।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The teachings of the Upanishads are revealed only to one who has supreme devotion to both God and the teacher. Without this devotion, the words remain mere words. This verse emphasizes the importance of bhakti (devotion) alongside jnana (knowledge).

verses.sanskrit:
अणोरणीयान् महतो महीयानात्मा गुहायां निहितोऽस्य जन्तोः |
तमक्रतुः पश्यति वीतशोको धातुप्रसादान्महिमानमात्मनः ||३.२०||
verses.transliteration:
aṇor aṇīyān mahato mahīyān ātmā guhāyāṃ nihito'sya jantoḥ |
tam akratuḥ paśyati vītaśoko dhātuprasādān mahimānam ātmanaḥ ||3.20||

word_meanings.meaning: hidden in the cave of the heart

verses.english_translation: Smaller than the smallest, greater than the greatest, the Self is hidden in the heart of this creature. One free from desire and grief, by the grace of the Creator, sees the glory of the Self.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अणोः अणीयान् — परमाणोरपि सूक्ष्मतरः। महतः महीयान् — महत्तत्त्वादपि महत्तरः। गुहायाम् — हृदयाकाशे — निहितः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Self is subtler than the subtlest and vaster than the vastest. It is hidden in the cave of the heart. Only one who is free from desire and sorrow, by the grace of the Lord, can perceive the glory of the Self. This verse also appears in the Katha Upanishad.
